skill.name: pitfalls-security
skill.description: Guides reviews of session keys, secret handling, cache safety, logging, and environment variable usage in security-sensitive code.
skill.summary: Review common security pitfalls in secrets, sessions, caching, logging, and environment variables.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, environment variable examples, browser storage examples, and private-key text in SKILL.md. AI review found these are documentation examples for defensive security guidance, with unsafe snippets explicitly marked as patterns to avoid. No prompt injection, executable payload, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command alerts correspond to Markdown code fences and inline example formatting, not executable Ruby or shell calls. The file is a single SKILL.md document and does not include runnable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows the flagged locations are TypeScript documentation blocks inside Markdown. There is no shell command invocation or executable file in the scanned content.

low_findings.title: Secret Handling Examples Are Defensive Guidance
low_findings.description: The environment variable, database URL, API key, localStorage, cache, and private-key references are examples used to teach safe and unsafe patterns. The unsafe examples are labeled with warnings such as NEVER and are not presented as code to run.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text repeatedly marks sensitive examples as unsafe and provides safer alternatives. No evidence found of collecting, logging, or transmitting real user secrets.

low_findings.title: Cryptography Alert Is Contextual Documentation
low_findings.description: The private-key and cryptography mentions describe security review topics and recommend AES-256-GCM for stored credentials. No weak cryptographic implementation or malicious key handling logic was found in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill text is framed as security guidance and contains no runnable weak-crypto routine. The static weak-cryptography blocker was not supported by the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: Security-sensitive code often leaks secrets through storage, caches, logs, or misused environment variables. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise checklist for finding and explaining those issues.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for plaintext private-key storage and unsafe browser storage patterns.
Checks session key designs for expiry, permission limits, and trade limits.
Identifies unsafe caching of user-specific sensitive data.
Reviews logging patterns for accidental secret exposure.
Checks frontend and backend environment variable usage patterns.
Suggests audit logging for sensitive operations.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not execute automated security scans.
It focuses on common application pitfalls, not full threat modeling.
It does not verify runtime configuration or deployed infrastructure.
It may need project-specific context for cryptographic key management choices.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge Security Review
use_cases.description: Review a pull request for secret storage, logging, cache, and session key mistakes before merge.

use_cases.title: Authentication Design Check
use_cases.description: Evaluate session key permissions, expiry, and limits during authentication feature design.

use_cases.title: Operational Logging Cleanup
use_cases.description: Find log statements that may expose configuration values, API keys, or sensitive user data.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Secret Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small code snippet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for unsafe secret storage, secret logging, and environment variable mistakes. Explain each issue in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Review Session Keys
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this session key implementation. Check expiry, permissions, maximum transaction limits, audit logging, and private-key handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Cache And Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these files for sensitive data in caches and logs. Separate confirmed issues from areas that need more context.

prompt_templates.title: Produce A Risk Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced security review for release readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release-readiness security report for this code. Cover session keys, cache safety, logs, environment variables, evidence, severity, and fixes.

output_examples.input: A review request for frontend code that stores a private key in browser storage.
output: High risk: private keys should not be stored in browser storage.
Recommended fix: use limited session keys with expiry and scoped permissions.
Add audit logging for key creation, rotation, and revocation events.

output_examples.input: A backend logging review that includes configuration and error objects.
output: Medium risk: configuration logging can expose API keys or database URLs.
Recommended fix: log environment names and request identifiers instead of full config objects.
Review error logging to avoid stack traces that include sensitive values.

output_examples.input: A cache review for trading and account data.
output: Low risk for public price data when short TTLs are used.
High risk for user-specific balances or private keys in shared caches.
Recommended fix: separate public cache entries from user-specific sensitive data.

best_practices: Ask for the smallest relevant code sample plus the surrounding security context.
Require evidence with file names and line numbers for every reported issue.
Treat examples marked as unsafe as teaching material, not implementation guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the assistant to approve private-key storage without a compensating control.
Do not paste real API keys, database URLs, or private keys into prompts.
Do not treat this checklist as a replacement for a full security review.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews common security pitfalls in session keys, secret storage, caches, logging, and environment variable usage.

faq.question: Does it run a scanner?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance for an AI assistant and depends on the code and context you provide.

faq.question: Can I paste real secrets for review?
faq.answer: No. Redact secrets before sharing code, logs, configuration, or environment variables.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for blockchain code?
faq.answer: No. Some examples mention trading and private keys, but the guidance applies to many application backends and frontends.

faq.question: Can it confirm my system is secure?
faq.answer: No. It can identify common issues, but final assurance requires testing, design review, and deployment context.
